The session makes clear the scope: "ponder skill first, feature create_design as follow-on." The commit signal was reached when the format spec was clear enough.


Now I'm looking at the existing milestones to figure out where to place this work. The highest milestone is v25-secrets-write-ui, so I'll use that as a reference point. For sizing, the first feature is straightforward — just updating the ponder skill template with the Design Artifact Protocol section. The second feature is the follow-on that extends the `create_design` action to generate HTML mockups, which will require touching the feature lifecycle code.

The third piece is the frontend scrapbook rendering to display those mockup files in an iframe. I'm thinking this is medium scope overall — probably one new milestone with two or three features bundled together. The naming could go a few ways: something like `v26-html-design-artifacts` or just `design-artifact-protocol`. The scrapbook UI piece is small enough that it could either be its own feature or fold into the HTML generation work. check what the highest number is: v25-secrets-write-ui. So next would be v26.

Looking at the scope more carefully, the three features have different sizes — the ponder skill update is small and self-contained, while the create_design HTML and scrapbook preview are both medium-sized frontend changes. I'll bundle them into one milestone `v26-html-design-artifacts` with three features tracking the ponder skill instruction update, the create_design HTML output, and the frontend inline rendering.

But wait, the session notes are explicit about sequencing: the ponder skill is the primary deliverable, and create_design is a follow-on. Given that clarity, I'll structure one milestone where the ponder skill is the main feature and the other two are dependent follow-ons.

input.content:
slug: v26-html-design-artifacts
title: HTML Design Artifacts — visual mockups replace prose design docs
vision: >
  When an agent designs a UI in a ponder session or creates a feature design,
  it produces a browser-openable HTML mockup instead of a Markdown prose
  description — making design review immediate and visual. Agents no longer
  write paragraphs describing layouts; they write layouts. Reviewers open a
  file and see the design, not imagine it.
features:
  - ponder-design-artifact-protocol
  - design-create-action-html
  - scrapbook-html-preview

text:
---

## Summary

**Milestone created:** `v26-html-design-artifacts` — *HTML Design Artifacts — visual mockups replace prose design docs*

**3 features created:**

| Slug | Title | Scope |
|---|---|---|
| `ponder-design-artifact-protocol` | Ponder Design Artifact Protocol — HTML mockups in ponder skill | **Primary** — add Design Artifact Protocol section to `sdlc_ponder.rs` template with format spec, filename convention (`*-mockup.html`), and capture instructions |
| `design-create-action-html` | Feature create_design action produces HTML mockup | **Follow-on** — extend the feature lifecycle `create_design` action to produce `design-mockup.html` instead of/alongside `design.md` |
| `scrapbook-html-preview` | Scrapbook renders `*-mockup.html` artifacts in an inline iframe | **Follow-on** — frontend: detect `*-mockup.html` in scrapbook and render via `<iframe>` instead of text |

**Ponder linked:** `ponder-design-improvements` → `committed_to: [v26-html-design-artifacts]`

**Next:**
```
/sdlc-prepare v26-html-design-artifacts
```
